plan_metadata.name: group  debug in teams sample msgext local

steps.description: Press the F1 key to open the command palette in Visual Studio Code, while viewing the "Preview README.md" document and Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit notifications.
Type 'Debug: Select and Start Debugging' into the VS Code Command Palette input field at the top of the window.
Press Enter to select "Debug: Select and Start Debugging" from the command palette dropdown in Visual Studio Code.
Click on the "Debug (Edge)" option in the Visual Studio Code debug configuration dropdown, highlighted by the blue selection bar within the command palette.
Click the "+ New resource group" option in the "Select a resource group" dropdown within Visual Studio Code, targeting the highlighted list item directly under the input field.
Type #text:${{var:app_name}}-${{var:random:###}}-rg  into the "New resource group name" input prompt at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Press the Enter key to confirm the new resource group name ("rg") in the input field at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface during Microsoft 365 agent setup.
Click the "Central US" option in the location dropdown within the "Location for the new resource group" dialog of Visual Studio Code, selecting the recommended region for the resource group setup.
Click the "Provision" button in the confirmation dialog within Visual Studio Code to initiate resource provisioning using the listed Azure and Microsoft 365 accounts.
Click on the "Password" input field in the Microsoft login screen to activate it for password entry.
Type ${{secret:M365_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD}} into the 'Password' field on the Microsoft login page for user ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME}}.
Press the "Enter" key to submit the login password in the Microsoft sign-in page form after entering the "Enter password" field.
Click the "Yes" button on the "Stay signed in?" prompt within the Microsoft login interface to confirm staying signed in.
Click the "Add" or "Open" button on the app details popup within the Microsoft Teams interface.
Click the "Open" button in the "Added successfully!" confirmation popup after installing the "search-extension-settings" app within the Microsoft Teams web interface.
